# awscc_wellarchitected_lens (Resource)

Definition of AWS::WellArchitected::Lens Resource Type



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `json_string` (String) The JSON representation of a lens.
- `lens_version` (String) The version of the lens.
- `tags` (Attributes List) The tags assigned to the lens. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) The description of the lens.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `lens_arn` (String) The ARN of the lens.
- `lens_id` (String) The unique identifier of the lens.
- `name` (String) The full name of the lens.
- `owner` (String) The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the lens.

<a id="nestedatt--tags"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tags`

Optional:

- `key` (String)
- `value` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wellarchitected_lens.example
  identity = {
    lens_arn = "lens_arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `lens_arn` (String) The ARN of the lens

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wellarchitected_lens.example
  id = "lens_arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_wellarchitected_lens.example "lens_arn"
```
